questions.question: After fixing B3, Y39 still has a discrepancy in B5. The region cell holds `Ra2bLd` (asymmetric, no trailing digit). The `code` field's B5 segment holds `Ra2bLd6` (extra trailing `6`). Which is correct — was the `6` a typo in the code field (then I should regenerate code from regions), or is the `6` meaningful and the region cell needs to become `Ra2bLd6`?

A1 has 55 singleton codes out of 89 unambiguous wolves (62%). The paper would treat singletons as evidence that this single region individually identifies most wolves. Do you want this stated as Tier-1 evidence, or should we condition the claim on orthogonal confirmation from at least one other region (e.g., C6)?

C6 (central nasal stripe) has 26 singletons out of 88 unambiguous and 42 distinct codes — the second-strongest single-region identifier after A1. For the paper, do we promote C6 alongside A1 in the headline, or treat it as a supporting region only?

D9 (side nape) has only 4 distinct codes total, no singletons, and a2 covers 40/100 wolves. For Figure 1, do we keep D9 in the main panel (to be transparent that one region failed) or move it to supplementary with a methods sentence explaining low discrimination?

D8 codes split into color-digit (after `a`) and pattern-digit (after `b`). 4 wolves have only the color part (e.g. `a4` with no `b` suffix) — they exist but pattern is genuinely uncoded. Should D8 stats report color-only and color+pattern separately, or treat them as one bucket?

The B-region grammar says trailing `a` = low contrast, `b` = high contrast. But ~30 wolves have B3/B4/B5 codes WITHOUT either suffix (e.g. M3 B3=`a3`, M2k B4=`c2`, Y43 B4=`d`). What does the absence encode — 'contrast not observable', 'default value', or 'no contrast info needed for this pattern'? This affects whether `a3` and `a3a` should merge in rank-frequency.

Y28's `code` field has B3=`PP` and B4=`PP` (doubled P), while the region columns have just `P`. Was the doubled-P meaningful (e.g., 'partly visible TWICE' or some emphasis), or just a transcription error?
Y31 C7: `code` field says `p` (lowercase), region cell says `P` (uppercase). Uppercase P = 'pattern unclear' status. Lowercase p has no defined meaning. Is the region cell `P` correct?

Y44 A2: `code` field says `a1i`, region cell says `a1u`. The letter `u` isn't in the A2 color set ({e..m}) or the standard pattern modifier set. Is `a1u` correct (and the color set should be extended), or a typo of `a1i`?

O82 has FOUR regions where `code` disagrees with the column cell, in a pattern suggesting the code's segments shifted. Region values: B3=`Pa`, B4=`N`, B5=`dl`, C6=`c`. Code claims: B3=`N`, B4=`Pa`, B5=`N`, C6=`dl`. Was the `code` written before some columns got re-labelled? Which set of values represents reality? (Also: B5=`dl` is unusual — not the standard B-grammar.)
In92 C6: `code` field says just `e` (one letter, color-only?), region cell says `c1f` (full pattern+color). These encode different amounts of information. Which is the true observation?

M6H D9: `code` field says `b2`, region cell says `a2`. D9 has only 4 distinct codes total (and `b2` isn't one of them) — so this is a meaningful disagreement. Was the original observation `a2` (one of the known 4) or `b2` (a NEW 5th code)?

The region columns themselves are fine — only the precomputed `code` string is off. Most likely a paste error. The QC panel has a 'Replace code with concatenated regions' row action — should we apply it to all 7 in one click? (Asks here for the methodology-level confirmation; per-row buttons are in QC.)

23 wolves are coded `social dynamic = 'lone'` (a 4th value not in the documented set {pack, group, unknown}). Y102 has `social dynamic = 'pack*'` (the only one). What does the asterisk mean — 'suspected pack but not confirmed', 'pack member seen alone', something else? Should it merge into `pack` or stay distinct?

Two wolves have `gender = '?'` (S19, F21) where the documented rule is 'blank when uncertain'. Should I set both to blank, or did the `?` encode 'we tried but couldn't tell' (different from 'never assessed')?

13 wolves total have at least one asymmetric region (R…L… encoding). In rank-frequency, do we count each asymmetric wolf as: (A) ONE entity at its raw code (`Ra3vzfLb1f`), (B) TWO entries (right + left as separate codes), or (C) hybrid: one in wolf-centric counts, two in side-aware view? The identification buckets are Unique / Shared 2-3 / 4-6 / 7-10 / 11-20 / 21-35 / 36+. Looking at the actual distribution, would you prefer to (A) keep as-is, (B) merge `Shared 11-20` and `Shared 21-35` into one `Shared 11-35` (since few codes land in each), or (C) shift boundaries to align with biological intuition (e.g., 'rare', 'uncommon', 'common', 'dominant')?

When you do clean: (A) which becomes the canonical column for the paper, (B) should the cleaned version be in English (for the paper) or Hebrew (for your field notes), (C) is the cleanup something I can help with by proposing groupings from the data?

We call codes `unambiguous` for status (well-defined codes). For the paper, would you prefer the published term to remain 'unambiguous', or shift to something like 'resolved', 'determinate', 'specific', or 'clean'?
